user_query:
Please build an event-triggered local automation that watches for new case watchlists and compiles structured findings from official sources, then produces a concise alert.

Use the provided files and produce all outputs under outputs/ as described. The Internet search step is required.

What to do:
- Modify config/watchlist.yaml to broaden official sources and candidate limits:
  - Add both "justice.gov" and "cftc.gov" under search.allowed_domains (keep any existing domains).
  - Set search.max_candidates to 3.
- Implement a watcher script that reacts when a new .jsonl file appears in the incoming/ directory. For this request, simulate the event by processing the provided incoming/cases.jsonl as if it were newly added.
- For each JSON record in incoming/cases.jsonl, perform search engine queries that combine the record's keywords with a domain restriction to the allowed domains from config/watchlist.yaml. Do not hardcode URLs. Only consider results whose URLs are on the allowed domains.
- For each case, return up to search.max_candidates total candidates (across all allowed domains combined). For each candidate page, capture:
  - source_domain (e.g., sec.gov, justice.gov, cftc.gov)
  - title (page title text)
  - url (full URL)
  - published_date (best-effort date parsed from the page or metadata; leave empty if unavailable)
  - retrieved_at (ISO-8601 timestamp when you accessed it)
- Write a structured JSON dossier to outputs/findings/cases_findings.json with this shape:
  {
    "processed_file": "incoming/cases.jsonl",
    "processed_at": "<ISO-8601 timestamp>",
    "allowed_domains": ["..."],
    "cases": [
      {
        "brief_name": "...",
        "query_terms": ["..."],
        "candidates": [ { "source_domain": "...", "title": "...", "url": "...", "published_date": "...", "retrieved_at": "..." } ]
      }
    ]
  }
  If a case has no matches, include it with candidates: [].
- Also write a CSV summary at outputs/summary/cases_summary.csv with one row per candidate in the JSON (omit cases with zero candidates). Columns: brief_name, source_domain, title, url, published_date, retrieved_at.
- Rewrite drafts/alert_draft.txt for tone, clarity, and brevity into a single-paragraph neutral academic note (<= 90 words) that mentions:
  - that official sources on the allowed domains were consulted,
  - the input file stem ("cases"),
  - and the two output paths (outputs/findings/cases_findings.json and outputs/summary/cases_summary.csv).
  Save this as outputs/alerts/cases_alert.txt.

Constraints/verification:
- The watcher must read allowed_domains and max_candidates from config/watchlist.yaml (your config edits must affect the run).
- The JSON and CSV must be consistent: the number of CSV rows must equal the total number of candidates across all cases in the JSON.
- Do not include direct URLs in this request; use a search engine to discover pages on the allowed official domains.

Deliverables to create:
- Modified config/watchlist.yaml with the domain additions and max_candidates: 3.
- The watcher/search code you add (e.g., under scripts/), which uses config/watchlist.yaml and performs live web searches.
- outputs/findings/cases_findings.json, outputs/summary/cases_summary.csv, and outputs/alerts/cases_alert.txt generated from incoming/cases.jsonl. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
